data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Injects Another $2B in CoreWeave to Speed Up Global AI Factory Buildout
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) has injected an additional $2 billion into AI cloud provider CoreWeave (CRWV) as part of an expanded partnership to speed up the construction of global AI factory infrastructure. The companies are collaborating to build AI factories, aiming for over 5 gigawatts of capacity by 2030.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As part of the deal, Nvidia purchased $2 billion of CoreWeave Class A common stock at $87.20 per share.

This investment deepens the already close relationship between the two, with CoreWeave serving as a specialized cloud provider for Nvidia's advanced GPUs (including future Rubin and Blackwell platforms).

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock based on 39 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $263.44 indicates 40.37% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Jumps 9% on $2B Nvidia Investment as Vera Chip Debuts
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is strengthening its grip on the AI world by sinking another $2 billion into CoreWeave (CRWV), one of its closest partners. This massive investment helps CoreWeave build out AI factories with enough power to rival five nuclear reactors. But the real surprise in this deal is the debut of Vera, a new Nvidia chip designed to challenge Intel (INTC) and AMD (AMD) for the "brain" of the data center.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia Attacks the CPU Market

For years, Nvidia has dominated the market for GPUs, the powerful chips that do the heavy lifting for AI. Now, with the launch of the Vera CPU, Nvidia is going after the host processors that have traditionally been sold by Intel and AMD. This is the first time Nvidia has offered a CPU on its own, meaning a company can now build a complete data center using only Nvidia-branded chips.

Jensen Huang described the Vera chip as "completely revolutionary" because it is built specifically to manage the fast-moving data that AI requires. By cutting out the need for chips from other companies, Nvidia can make sure its systems work faster and use less power. This move puts immediate pressure on traditional chip giants who are already struggling to keep up with the AI boom.

CoreWeave Builds the Power Grid

CoreWeave is using the new $2 billion from Nvidia to fuel an incredible construction project. The goal is to reach 5 gigawatts of computing capacity by 2030, enough electricity to power nearly 4 million homes. CoreWeave acts as a specialized neocloud, providing the massive clusters of chips that startups like OpenAI and xAI need to train their next-generation models.

This partnership is about more than just money; it is a deep engineering bond. CoreWeave will be the first to test and use Nvidia's latest tech, including the Vera Rubin superchip platform. While some investors worry about the circular nature of Nvidia investing in its own customers, the company argues that this is the only way to build the massive amount of infrastructure the world currently demands.

Investors Balance Risk and Reward

The news sent CoreWeave's stock climbing 11% as it helps ease worries about the company's heavy debt. CoreWeave has been spending billions of dollars on land and power faster than it brings in revenue, which has made some traders nervous. Having the world's most valuable company as a major shareholder provides a safety net that suggests CoreWeave's aggressive growth plan is working.

Meanwhile, Nvidia's own forecast for 2026 remains incredibly bullish. The company expects to see half a trillion dollars in revenue from data center chips alone by the end of next year. By expanding into CPUs and storage, Nvidia is making sure that even if the competition gets better at making GPUs, they will still have to pay Nvidia for the rest of the computer.

data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Mixed as Investors Await Big Tech Earnings, Gold Tops $5,100
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed on Monday morning, as investors look forward to Big Tech earnings this week, including reports from Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), and Apple (AAPL). Electric vehicle (EV) maker Tesla (TSLA) will also announce its Q4 2025 results this week. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.23% and 0.06%, respectively, while futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up by 0.02% at 8:15 a.m. EST on January 26.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, gold touched a fresh all-time high, crossing $5,100 an ounce on Monday. The yellow metal extended its stellar run as investors favored the safe-haven asset amid geopolitical woes related to Greenland and Venezuela, as well as global volatility. Moreover, the trade tensions between the U.S. and Canada have resurfaced. U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to slap 100% tariffs on Canada if it signs a trade deal with China. However, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Sunday that his country has no intention of entering into a free trade agreement with Beijing.

All eyes are on key earnings reports from over 90 S&P 500 companies this week, including Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Apple, and Tesla. Other major earnings include reports from Boeing (BA), UnitedHealth (UNH), AT&T (T), International Business Machines (IBM), Starbucks (SBUX), ASML Holding (ASML), ServiceNow (NOW), Visa (V), Mastercard (MA), SoFi Technologies (SOFI), Exxon Mobil (XOM), and Chevron (CVX).

Investors will also focus on the U.S. Federal Reserve's first policy decision of 2026 on Wednesday. While the central bank is expected to keep its overnight rate intact, traders will look for insights into the Fed officials' plans for potential rate cuts ahead.

Turning to trending stocks, USA Rare Earth (USAR) shares surged 14% in Monday's pre-market trading after the Trump administration took a stake in the rare earths miner. The company will issue 16.1 million shares of common stock and 17.6 million shares in warrants. Also, Allied Gold (AAUC) stock was up about 4%, as the gold miner agreed to be acquired by Hong Kong-based Zijin Gold for C$5.5 billion in cash.

Additionally, Newmont (NEM) stock was up 4.6% in pre-market trading as gold prices continued to trend higher amid geopolitical uncertainty. In contrast, Enphase Energy (ENPH) shares declined more than 2% after the solar energy company said that it would cut its workforce by more than 5%. Plus, Revolution Medicines (RVMD) stock plunged nearly 20% after the Wall Street Journal reported that Merck (MRK) ended its talks to acquire the cancer-drug biotech.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: RTX and Northrop Grumman Earnings Preview: Trump's Defense Push and Geopolitical Tensions Drive Investor Focus
data.description:

Also, investors will be watching how Trump's renewed focus on defense spending and rising geopolitical tensions, especially from conflicts in Eastern Europe to rising security concerns in the Indo-Pacific, are shaping contract pipelines and profits.

data.title: Nvidia Stock Defies Logic with 58% Gain Since DeepSeek Panic. Here's Why.
data.description:
One year after the Chinese startup DeepSeek caused a massive market panic, the global AI boom has proven to be much more resilient than skeptics expected. Currently, tech giants and chipmakers like Nvidia (NVDA) are trading at or near record highs, showing that the fear of cheap AI replacing expensive hardware was largely a temporary distraction. While some called DeepSeek a permanent shift in the industry, the last 12 months have shown that the demand for high-end computing power is actually growing faster than ever.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia Rebounds from Historic Wipeout

Exactly one year ago, DeepSeek's new AI model erased a record $589 billion from Nvidia's market value in a single day. At the time, investors feared that because DeepSeek built a powerful model for a fraction of the usual cost, nobody would need Nvidia's expensive chips anymore. This knee-jerk reaction sent shockwaves through the S&P 500 (SPX), dragging down tech, energy, and utility stocks alike.

However, the panic didn't last long, and Nvidia's growth has since reached levels that defy all logic. The company's stock is up 58% since that sell-off, recently helping it hit a $5 trillion valuation. Experts now agree that while DeepSeek showed AI can be made more efficiently, the most advanced models still require the massive scaling that only thousands of top-tier GPUs can provide.

Hyperscalers Double Down on Spending

One of the biggest fears during the DeepSeek era was that giant companies like Microsoft (MSFT) and Meta (META) would slash their budgets for AI hardware. The opposite has happened, with capital expenditures (capex) expected to reach roughly $600 billion in 2026. This money is being used to build massive data centers that serve as the foundation for both internal projects and third-party AI services.

This wave of spending has broadened the AI trade to include more than just chipmakers. Investors are now piling into memory stocks like Micron (MU) and Western Digital (WDC), as well as energy providers like Constellation Energy (CEG). These companies provide the physical parts and power needed to keep AI brains running.

Analysts Debate the China Threat

At the 2026 World Economic Forum in Davos, Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis described the DeepSeek craze as a "massive overreaction." He noted that while Chinese firms are excellent at catching up to current technology, they have yet to show they can innovate past the frontier set by American labs. This gap suggests that U.S. tech leadership remains secure for the foreseeable future.

Even so, the DeepSeek moment served as a reminder that technology moves incredibly fast. While Nvidia still dominates the market, other companies like Alphabet (GOOGL) and Broadcom (AVGO) are successfully launching their own custom AI chips. This evolution means that while the need for computing power is infinite, the ways that power is delivered are becoming more diverse and competitive.

data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Flat Ahead of Key Tech Earnings and Fed Meeting
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were steady early Monday morning as investors got ready for a busy week filled with major earnings reports and a key Federal Reserve meeting. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were down 0.23%, 0.02%, and 0.1%, respectively, at 7:50 a.m. EDT on January 26. Interestingly, gold touched a fresh all-time high, crossing $5,100 an ounce on Monday.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

U.S. stocks finished the week mixed as gains in technology shares helped offset weakness in financial stocks. The DJIA fell 0.58%, the S&P 500 ended nearly flat, up 0.03%, and the Nasdaq 100 outperformed, rising 0.34%.

Earnings will be a major focus this week. More than 90 companies in the S&P 500 are set to report results, including Apple (AAPL), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT). The earnings season has begun on a strong note, with about three out of four reporting companies beating Wall Street expectations, according to FactSet.

However, some stocks moved lower after earnings, including Intel (INTC) and Netflix (NFLX), as investors reacted to weaker guidance and cautious outlooks despite headline results.

Attention will now shift to the Federal Reserve. The central bank is expected to announce its first policy decision of the year on Wednesday. Rates are expected to remain unchanged, with investors watching for signals on future cuts.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/26/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Mixed Ahead of Big Tech Earnings, Gold Hits Record High
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed early Monday ahead of Big Tech earnings this week, including reports from Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), and Apple (AAPL). Electric vehicle (EV) maker Tesla (TSLA) will also announce its Q4 2025 results this week. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) were down 0.06%, while futures tied to the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.02% and 0.03%, respectively, at 3:59 a.m. EST on January 26.

Interestingly, gold touched a fresh all-time high, crossing $5,100 an ounce on Monday. The yellow metal extended its stellar run as investors favored the safe-haven asset amid geopolitical woes related to Greenland and Venezuela, as well as global volatility. Moreover, the trade tensions between the U.S. and Canada have resurfaced. U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to slap 100% tariffs on Canada if it signs a trade deal with China. However, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Sunday that his country has no intention of entering into a free trade agreement with Beijing.

During Friday's regular trading, the three major averages closed mixed. The Nasdaq Composite gained 0.28% while the S&P 500 ended the session with a modest rise of 0.03%. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones fell by 0.58%. Nevertheless, the three major indices ended the week in the red. The Dow Jones and S&P 500 declined 0.5% and 0.4%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite lost less than 0.1% last week.

All eyes are on key earnings reports from over 90 S&P 500 companies this week, including Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Apple, and Tesla. Other major earnings include reports from Boeing (BA), UnitedHealth (UNH), AT&T (T), International Business Machines (IBM), Starbucks (SBUX), ASML Holding (ASML), ServiceNow (NOW), Visa (V), Mastercard (MA), SoFi Technologies (SOFI), Exxon Mobil (XOM), and Chevron (CVX).

Investors will also focus on the U.S. Federal Reserve's first policy decision of 2026 on Wednesday. While the central bank is expected to keep its overnight rate intact, traders will look for insights into the Fed officials' plans for potential rate cuts ahead.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.205% at the time of writing. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $60.94 per barrel as of the last check. In contrast, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to over $5,097 per ounce as of writing.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened slightly positive today, as investors closely watched developments on the geopolitical front.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on Monday as traders assessed geopolitical concerns.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 0.06%. However, in China, the Shanghai Composite and Shenzhen Component closed lower by 0.09% and 0.85%, respectively. Also, Japan's Nikkei fell 1.79%, while the Topix declined 2.13%.

data.title: Cathie Wood Loads Up on Broadcom and Crypto Stocks, Trims Meta by $8M Ahead of Q4. Here's Why
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) disclosed their daily ETF trades for Friday, January 23, 2026. The firm added to chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) and several crypto-linked and AI stocks, while trimming exposure to Meta Platforms (META) and select genomics and defense names.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Stocks Did Wood Buy on January 23?

1. Broadcom Stock (AVGO) – Wood made her largest buy of the day in Broadcom, purchasing 49,048 shares worth about $15.96 million through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW), signaling confidence in the company's AI and data-center exposure. Broadcom shares fell 1.7% in the prior session, pressured by broader chip-sector weakness after Intel (INTC) issued a cautious outlook.

2. Coinbase Stock (COIN) – Wood also added 42,179 shares of Coinbase, valued at about $9.41 million, through ARKK and ARK Fintech Innovation ETF (ARKF). The move extends ARK's ongoing bet on crypto infrastructure and digital asset platforms. Meanwhile, COIN stock fell 2.8%, mainly due to weakness in the broader crypto market.

3. Circle Stock (CRCL) – ARK bought 129,446 shares of Circle for about $9.24 million, mainly through the ARKK ETF, with a smaller portion added via the ARKF ETF. The buy reflects continued interest in digital payments and stablecoin-related businesses.

4. Bullish Stock (BLSH) – Cathie Wood added 88,533 shares of Bullish, investing around $3.23 million. Most of the shares were bought through the ARKK ETF, with additional shares purchased via the ARK Fintech Innovation ETF.

5. WeRide Stock (WRD) – Wood has shown a strong interest in autonomous vehicle (AV) technology. The ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ) purchased 180,740 shares of Chinese ride-hailing service platform WeRide, totaling $1.64 million. The robotaxi company has been expanding its fleet and operations in Beijing and Guangzhou.

To compare how these newly added positions stack up, let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Leans on AI and Azure in Its Quantum Strategy
data.description:
Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) is refining its approach to quantum computing by placing greater emphasis on software tools rather than physical machines. The company recently shared updates to its Quantum Development Kit, which is designed to help developers work with early quantum systems using familiar tools.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the center of the effort is a shift from fragile physical qubits toward logical qubits, which are built to reduce errors. According to Microsoft, progress in quantum computing now depends as much on software as it does on hardware.

Matthias Troyer, a technical fellow and corporate vice president at Microsoft, said reaching fault-tolerant systems will require strong tools that help researchers design and test programs today. He noted that software must prepare developers for larger systems that remain years away.

Microsoft said its goal is to make quantum work feel closer to normal software development. As a result, the Quantum Development Kit runs on standard laptops and integrates with Visual Studio Code and GitHub Copilot.

In the meantime, MSFT shares rose 3.28% on Friday, closing at $465.95.
data.author: Ran Melamed
